Application For Entry Of Default Judgment Against Defendants Bay Decking Co., Inc., And Charles Lowe Company
Off Calendar Sep-11-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter came on calendar this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing as to defendants Bay Decking Co., Inc. and Charles Lowe Company.
Dept. 514. Judge: Eric R. Fleming. Clerk: Kevin Dougherty, Reporter: Mitch L. Lyon, CSR #6840. Telephone: 415-551-3703.

James H. Ghilotti, Esq. appeared for plaintiffs Arthur Laurey and Frances Laurey, who were not present at this morning's hearing.
No appearance by defaulted defendants Bay Decking Co., Inc. or Charles Lowe Company.
Plaintiffs' complaint was filed on May 29, 2014 and served on defendant Bay Decking Co., Inc. on May 5, 2016.
Statement of Damages was served on defendant Bay Decking Co., Inc. on May 5, 2016 and filed on June 23, 2016.
Default was entered against defendant Bay Decking Co., Inc. on JUne 23, 2016.
Order waiving witness appearance at default prove-up hearing was filed on August 9, 2018.

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of Plaintiffs Arthur Laurey and Frances Laurey, and against the Defaulted Defendant Bay Decking Co., Inc., as follows:

Economic Damages for plaintiff Arthur Laurey - $542,525.99.
Non-Economic Damages for plaintiff Arthur Laurey - $10,000,000.00.
Loss of Consortium as to plaintiff Frances Laurey - $10,000,000.00.
Total Economic & Non-Economic Damages as to defendant Bay Decking Co., Inc. - $20,542,525.00.

Plaintiffs' complaint was filed on May 29, 2014 and served on defendant Charles Lowe Company on May 29, 2014.
Statement of Damages was served on defendant Charles Lowe Company on April 29, 2016 and filed on July 6, 2016.
Default was entered against defendant Charles Lowe Company on July 6, 2016.
Order waiving witness appearance at default prove-up hearing was filed on August 9, 2018.

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of Plaintiffs Arthur Laurey and Frances Laurey, and against the Defaulted Defendant Charles Lowe Company, as follows:

Economic Damages for plaintiff Arthur Laurey - $542,525.99.
Non-Economic Damages for plaintiff Arthur Laurey - $10,000,000.00.
Loss of Consortium as to plaintiff Frances Laurey - $10,000,000.00.
Total Economic & Non-Economic Damages as to defendant Charles Lowe Company - $20,542,525.00.

Proposed Judgment was submitted to and signed by the Court.

RULING:
DEFENDANT FRASER'S BOILER SERVICE, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Fraser's Boiler Service, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Laurey was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Plaintiffs' responses to defendant's special interrogatories were not factually devoid, they properly identified Charles James as a person with knowledge of facts supporting plaintiffs' claims, and defendant failed to demonstrate that Mr. James does not have such knowledge. This failure precludes defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826. Defendant has withdrawn its argument based on the sophisticated user defense. Order signed in open court. Judge: Garrett L. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826. While Mr. Laurey's deposition testimony demonstrates that defendant's employees performed work in his presence, defendant sustained its burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that any product or material to which defendant's employees exposed Mr. Laurey contained asbestos.
